description: SUBSTANTIAL HISTORIC RENOVATION OF EXISTING FULLY SPRINKLERED 3-STORY BRICK MASONRY AND WOOD MUSEUM (CULTURAL FACILITY). NO CHANGE OF USE IS PROPOSED. THE CAMPUS IS COMPRISED OF SEVERAL BUILDINGS THAT HAVE BEEN ACQUIRED OVER TIME THAT ARE INTERCONNECTED. 2 AREAS OF NEW CONSTRUCTION WILL SLIGHTLY EXPAND INTO EXISTING NON-PERMEABLE COURTYARDS AND INCREASE THE OVERALL GROSS SQUARE FOOTAGE. STORMWATER WILL BE RETAINED IN UNDERGROUND TANKS WITHIN 2 COURTYARDS. THE SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDES SEVERAL NEW ELEVATORS AND REGRADING OF THE SITE TO MAKE THE CAMPUS ADA ACCESSIBLE. STRUCTURAL STABILIZATION WILL BE PERFORMED THROUGHOUT ALL BUILDINGS. THE CAMPUS WILL RECEIVE NEW MECHANICAL, ELECTRICAL, SPRINKLER, FIRE ALARM, STORMWATER, AND PLUMBING SYSTEMS. THE PROPERTIES WERE RESUBDIVIDED INTO ONE LOT OF RECORD AND CONSIDERED ONE BUILDING BY CODE. ALL WORK TO BE DONE AS PER PLANS.

description: Odyssey House Louisana Tonti St. Campus is a Residential Care Facility (rehabilitative recovery) occupying various buildings on the site at 1125 N. Tonti St. This project is a renovation of Building A, which is a 3-story-plus-attic historic building built in 1895. Project scope is to renovate the building to accommodate common areas and administrative use on Level 1, sleeping units on Level 2 and 3, and common space and mechanical on Level 4 (attic). Scope also includes site work including the execution of the remaining stormwater management plan work as submitted with Building E (permit ref code GHY0E6, no. 22-30761-RNVS).

Parking will provided in the rear and the 10' buffer yard shall be provided as required per the CZO.
